text: So, to recap, we agreed to protect the margin and move fast.
Today you take that one step further: you sit at the table and negotiate the deal itself.

Thanks for meeting, Rachel. Let us find the terms that work for both sides. Where do you see our common ground?
We agree on the timeline. The gap is the price, and the exclusivity clause.
If you can move on price, we will commit to a longer term. That trade-off protects us both.
Interesting. So your counteroffer is a lower price for two years instead of one?
Exactly. But I have to be honest: no exclusivity is a deal-breaker for us.
Understood. If we settle the price today, I will take the clause to my board. Do we have a deal?

Carlos, before you walk into this negotiation, let me give you a quick brief. The other side wants to close fast, so that is your leverage on timing. Know your bottom line: the lowest price you can accept and still protect the margin. Do not open with it. Start a little higher and leave room to bargain. If they push hard on price, do not just say no. Offer a trade-off, a lower price in exchange for a longer contract. Look for common ground early, the points you both want, and build from there. And remember the one deal-breaker we discussed: we will not sign without the exclusivity clause. If they refuse that, you are ready to walk away. Stay calm, listen first, and let them make the first counteroffer.

Hi Carlos, this is Rachel. Thanks for a productive session today. I wanted to leave a short recap of where we stand. We found common ground on the timeline, so that part is settled. On price, we are still a little apart, but your counteroffer was reasonable, and I think we can close the gap. My team liked the trade-off you proposed, a lower price for a two-year term instead of one. The exclusivity clause is still the hard part for us, and I will not pretend otherwise. Let me take it to my board and come back to you on Thursday. If we can agree on the terms by Friday, I am confident we have a deal. Talk soon, and thanks again for keeping it constructive.
